provenance.source_corpus: Comparison of Site Preparation and Revegetation Strategies Within a Sphagnum-dominated Peatland Following Removal of an Oil Well Pad

source_spans.text: areas that were rough had greater species richness under natural recovery
diversity tended to be lower on smooth plots (1.994 ± 0.158) compared with those having rough microtopography (2.258 ± 0.158)
reduced the presence of some native vegetation

survival and growth of woody transplants (Picea mariana—black spruce and Ledum groenlandicum—Labrador tea) were greater when planted within the top and middle microtopographic positions rather than micro-depressions
survival of this species lower in areas receiving acrotelm
sedge transplant cover remained significantly lower within the top microtopographic position compared to the middle and bottom positions

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:0b427c6e5e23
edges.original_sentence: Although overall survival of transplants was similar between areas smoothed and left rough, areas that were rough had greater species richness under natural recovery, including trees, shrubs, and other perennial herbs.

source_spans.text: Although overall survival of transplants was similar between areas smoothed and left rough, areas that were rough had greater species richness under natural recovery, including trees, shrubs, and other perennial herbs.

source_document.title: Comparison of Site Preparation and Revegetation Strategies Within a Sphagnum-dominated Peatland Following Removal of an Oil Well Pad

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:0b427c6e5e23
edges.original_sentence: Shannon’s diversity under natural recovery was significantly affected by surface roughness (ANOV A; F1,11 = 2.94, p = 0.10): diversity tended to be lower on smooth plots (1.994 ± 0.158) compared with those having rough microtopography (2.258 ± 0.158).

source_spans.text: Shannon’s diversity under natural recovery was significantly affected by surface roughness (ANOV A; F1,11 = 2.94, p = 0.10): diversity tended to be lower on smooth plots (1.994 ± 0.158) compared with those having rough microtopography (2.258 ± 0.158).

source_document.title: Comparison of Site Preparation and Revegetation Strategies Within a Sphagnum-dominated Peatland Following Removal of an Oil Well Pad

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:88d1df434071
edges.original_sentence: Contrary to expectations, no benefits of acrotelm application were found on vegetation recovery during the first two seasons, and even reduced the presence of some native vegetation.

source_spans.text: Contrary to expectations, no benefits of acrotelm application were found on vegetation recovery during the first two seasons, and even reduced the presence of some native vegetation.

source_document.title: Comparison of Site Preparation and Revegetation Strategies Within a Sphagnum-dominated Peatland Following Removal of an Oil Well Pad

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:56c84aaef3ff
edges.original_sentence: Moreover, survival and growth of woody transplants (Picea mariana—black spruce and Ledum groenlandicum—Labrador tea) were greater when planted within the top and middle microtopographic positions rather than micro-depressions.

source_spans.text: Moreover, survival and growth of woody transplants (Picea mariana—black spruce and Ledum groenlandicum—Labrador tea) were greater when planted within the top and middle microtopographic positions rather than micro-depressions.

source_document.title: Comparison of Site Preparation and Revegetation Strategies Within a Sphagnum-dominated Peatland Following Removal of an Oil Well Pad

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:88d1df434071
edges.original_sentence: Ledum groenlandicum was additionally affected significantly by acrotelm application during the first growing season (ANOV A; F1,14 = 4.18, p = 0.048), with survival of this species lower in areas receiving acrotelm (50%) compared to areas without acrotelm (60.4%).

source_spans.text: Ledum groenlandicum was additionally affected significantly by acrotelm application during the first growing season (ANOV A; F1,14 = 4.18, p = 0.048), with survival of this species lower in areas receiving acrotelm (50%) compared to areas without acrotelm (60.4%).

source_document.title: Comparison of Site Preparation and Revegetation Strategies Within a Sphagnum-dominated Peatland Following Removal of an Oil Well Pad

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:5bd3abd5ba36
edges.original_sentence: Overall, species richness and diversity were marginally greater with the introduction of transplanted species, apparently as a direct result of the additional species installed.

source_spans.text: Overall, species richness and diversity were marginally greater with the introduction of transplanted species, apparently as a direct result of the additional species installed.

source_document.title: Comparison of Site Preparation and Revegetation Strategies Within a Sphagnum-dominated Peatland Following Removal of an Oil Well Pad

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:81992d906e08
edges.original_sentence: In contrast, sedge transplant cover remained significantly lower ( p < 0.05) within the top microtopographic position (2.8%) compared to the middle (3.9%) and bottom (4.0%) positions.

source_spans.text: In contrast, sedge transplant cover remained significantly lower ( p < 0.05) within the top microtopographic position (2.8%) compared to the middle (3.9%) and bottom (4.0%) positions.
